mnemonics: Zorn Orders Rightly: Maximal chains lead to maximal elements, guaranteeing choice in sets.
keyPoints: Zorn's Lemma states that if every chain in a partially ordered set has an upper bound, then the set has a maximal element.
The Axiom of Choice asserts that given any collection of non-empty sets, it is possible to select one element from each set.
Zorn's Lemma, the Axiom of Choice, and the Well-Ordering Principle are equivalent in set theory.
commonMistakes: Assuming Zorn's Lemma applies to any ordered set (it requires every chain to have an upper bound).
Confusing maximal elements with maximum elements (a maximal element is not necessarily the largest, but one with nothing greater than it).
